page_data.page_title: Right-Sized Assets in Freight Logistics | WARP
page_data.meta_description: Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency. Get an instant quote from WARP.

hero.headline: Right-Sized Assets in Freight Logistics
hero.subhead: Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

overview.heading: What Is Right-Sized Assets?
overview.content: Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

how_it_works.heading: How Right-Sized Assets Works
how_it_works.content: WARP's fleet spans cargo vans through 53-foot trailers. Each shipment is matched to the smallest vehicle that fits the load, so shippers don't pay for empty trailer space. Multi-stop routes may use different vehicle types across stops.

warp_implementation.heading: How WARP Uses Right-Sized Assets
points: Vehicle selection from cargo van to 53-foot trailer based on cube and weight
No paying for unused trailer space on partial loads
Mixed fleet deployment across route stops
Automatic vehicle upgrade/downgrade based on volume changes

when_to_use.heading: When to Use Right-Sized Assets
when_to_use.content: Critical for shippers with variable load sizes, especially retail replenishment where pallet counts differ per store. Also valuable for shippers currently overpaying for full trailers on partial loads.

solutions.label: Store Replenishment
solutions.description: Scheduled multi-stop retail freight delivery using LTL and right-sized assets

equipment.label: Cargo Van
equipment.description: Enclosed cargo van for small-freight and last-mile delivery

equipment.label: Box Truck
equipment.description: Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes

equipment.label: 53-Foot Trailer
equipment.description: Standard full-size dry van trailer for high-volume linehaul and FTL freight

concepts.label: Flexible Routing
concepts.description: Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements

items.q: What is right-sized assets in freight?
items.a: Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

WARP's fleet spans cargo vans through 53-foot trailers. Each shipment is matched to the smallest vehicle that fits the load, so shippers don't pay for empty trailer space. Multi-stop routes may use different vehicle types across stops.

items.q: When should a shipper use right-sized assets?
items.a: Critical for shippers with variable load sizes, especially retail replenishment where pallet counts differ per store. Also valuable for shippers currently overpaying for full trailers on partial loads.

items.q: How does WARP implement right-sized assets?
items.a: Vehicle selection from cargo van to 53-foot trailer based on cube and weight. No paying for unused trailer space on partial loads. Mixed fleet deployment across route stops.

items.q: What metrics matter for right-sized assets?
items.a: Key performance metrics include cube utilization, cost per pallet, vehicle match accuracy, deadhead reduction.

rendered.body_text:
Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

WARP's fleet spans cargo vans through 53-foot trailers. Each shipment is matched to the smallest vehicle that fits the load, so shippers don't pay for empty trailer space. Multi-stop routes may use different vehicle types across stops.

Applies to LTL, Cargo Van / Box Truck freight operations. Learn how WARP implements this at wearewarp.com/quote.
rendered.primary_content_html:
What Is Right-Sized Assets?

Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

Applies to: LTL, Cargo Van / Box Truck

How Right-Sized Assets Works

WARP's fleet spans cargo vans through 53-foot trailers. Each shipment is matched to the smallest vehicle that fits the load, so shippers don't pay for empty trailer space. Multi-stop routes may use different vehicle types across stops.

How WARP Uses Right-Sized Assets

  • Vehicle selection from cargo van to 53-foot trailer based on cube and weight
  • No paying for unused trailer space on partial loads
  • Mixed fleet deployment across route stops
  • Automatic vehicle upgrade/downgrade based on volume changes

When to Use Right-Sized Assets

Critical for shippers with variable load sizes, especially retail replenishment where pallet counts differ per store. Also valuable for shippers currently overpaying for full trailers on partial loads.

Right-Sized Assets Performance Metrics

  • Cube Utilization
  • Cost Per Pallet
  • Vehicle Match Accuracy
  • Deadhead Reduction

Related Solutions & Capabilities

Solutions

  • Store Replenishment — Scheduled multi-stop retail freight delivery using LTL and right-sized assets

Equipment

  • Cargo Van — Enclosed cargo van for small-freight and last-mile delivery
  • Box Truck — Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes
  • 53-Foot Trailer — Standard full-size dry van trailer for high-volume linehaul and FTL freight

Related Concepts

  • Flexible Routing — Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements

Ship with Right-Sized Assets

Get Instant Quote Book a Fit Call
